text: This is awesome. Watch this comment, smash into Jupiter. That is Shoemaker-loving nine. Well, pieces of it. Jupiter's gravity torred to shreds. Over six days, 21 pieces pummeled the planet and left a few scars. So when this happened it made people think what if that had hit Earth? Turns out Jupiter is taking a lot of hits for us. They call it the vacuum cleaner of the solar system because it's so massive compared to the other planets that its tidal force is so strong it tends to rip objects out of orbit, pulling them at incredible speeds into its own orbit, taking hit after hit after hit. Well, we sit peacefully over here. But sometimes it actually flings those objects at us. So to learn how we defend ourselves when Jupiter doesn't, follow along.
